app_upgrade_body: Reopen the app to finish loading the new version.
background_help_banner_body: Exempt KiekR from battery optimization so it can reconnect while the screen is off.
background_help_banner_title: Connection keeps dropping in the background?
chat_addtoapp_body: This channel isn't in the app yet. Add it first, then install it on the connected node to send.
chat_clear_confirm_body: %1$d messages will be deleted. This cannot be undone.
chat_connect_node_message: Pair with a node in My node to see your chats and channels.
chat_delete_dm_confirm_body: %1$d messages will be deleted. This cannot be undone.
chat_detail_channel_unsigned_advisory: Channel messages are unsigned — the sender name can be anyone in the channel.
chat_detail_directory_offline_caption: Online directory is off — enable a source in Settings → Sharing → Directory.
chat_detail_hop_candidates_explain: Several repeaters match this hop's short path prefix. We can't pick the right one automatically — choose the one that was on your path.
chat_detail_hop_not_found: no match — directory had no entry for this prefix
chat_detail_hop_outside_radius: no match — closest online hits sit outside the sanity radius
chat_detail_map_footer: Draws the repeater chain on a map. Hops without a heard advert show as unresolved.
chat_detail_path_backfill_footer: This message was drained from the paired node's offline queue. The device doesn't report the on-air hop chain, so the path can't be reconstructed.
chat_detail_path_footer_bytes: Each hop is the first %1$d byte(s) of a repeater's public key. Short keys may match multiple repeaters — tap the globe to look up missing hops in the online directory.
chat_detail_repeats_footer: When a repeater rebroadcasts your message, you'll see it here.
chat_discovered_bulk_ignore_confirm_mess: This stops prompts for all %1$d discovered channels. You can still add them later from Settings → Channels.
chat_discovered_channels_footer: Tap to inspect, then Add or Ignore.
chat_dm_empty_footer: Tap a node in Nodes → Send message to start a thread.
chat_hidden_channel_action_footer: Add surfaces the channel in your chat list and settings. Ignore silences future prompts for this key.
chat_hidden_channel_key_footer: Tap the key to copy. Channels are matched by key, not name — collisions are common.
chat_install_full_body: All slots are taken. Remove an installed channel or contact from Settings first.
chat_install_not_installed_body: Stock-firmware nodes need the channel/peer in a slot before they can TX.
chat_room_compose_guest_hint: Sign in as a member with write access to post.
chat_room_directory_footer: Tap Look up to query the online directory for this 4-byte author key.
chat_stray_cleanup_plural: Cleaned up %1$d stray messages from non-person contacts.
chat_stray_cleanup_singular: Cleaned up %1$d stray message from non-person contacts.
map_empty_hint_adjust_filters: Try widening or clearing the active filters.
map_empty_hint_no_gps: Adverts with GPS coordinates land here as they're heard.
map_empty_hint_no_type_gps: No %1$s of this type advertise GPS coordinates yet.
map_filter_hops_hint: 0 = only nodes heard directly by the paired device. Nodes with unknown hop counts are always shown.
map_path_no_path_body: None of this message's repeaters — and no origin — have GPS coordinates stored. Close this view, tap the globe icon on the message's path section, then come back.
mynode_broadcast_advert_footer: Other nodes need our advert in their contact table before they can decrypt our DMs. Tap to pick reach — zero-hop is quiet (neighbours only); flood is loud (whole mesh).
mynode_clock_ahead_body: The node's clock is set ahead of your phone. Adverts and admin commands carry a future timestamp and are silently dropped by the mesh's anti-replay guard.
mynode_clock_ahead_rtc_body: This device has a battery-backed RTC. Software can't reset it — flash firmware or remove the RTC backup battery to recover.
mynode_config_error_pin_rejected: The node rejected the new PIN.
mynode_config_error_radio_rejected: The node rejected the radio config (%.3f MHz · BW %.1f kHz · SF %d · CR 4/%d).
mynode_config_error_range: value was out of the firmware-enforced range
mynode_config_error_suffix: %1$s Reason: %2$s. The previous value remains active.
mynode_connect_via_wifi_footer: For ESP32 boards running companion firmware built with WIFI_SSID. Enter the device's IP and TCP port.
mynode_device_actions_footer: Reboot restarts the paired node. Factory reset wipes the device's contacts, channels, and prefs — the app's local data is not touched.
mynode_dialog_broadcast_advert_body: Other nodes need our advert before they can decrypt our DMs. Pick reach:
mynode_disconnect_footer: Drops the link. Reconnect any time from the companion list above.
mynode_disconnect_wifi_footer: Closes the TCP link. Reconnect any time from the WiFi entry below.
mynode_factory_reset_dialog_body: Wipes contacts, channels, regions, prefs, and identity on the device. Irreversible — there's no undo. Export anything you want to keep before continuing.
mynode_flood_body: Whole mesh. Loud — every repeater rebroadcasts. Use sparingly; flood adverts pay airtime per repeater.
mynode_forget_dialog_body: Unpairs and clears the cached identifier. The node won't be auto-reconnected on next app launch. You'll need to re-pair to use it again.
mynode_forget_footer: Forget unpairs and clears the cached identifier. The node must be re-paired to reconnect.
mynode_identity_footer: DMs are signed with this identity. Adopted from the paired node when you first connect; persists across reconnects until you adopt a different node's seed.
mynode_lora_advanced_body_off: Advanced exposes raw freq / BW / SF / CR for configs outside the catalogue.
mynode_lora_advanced_body_on: Advanced writes raw freq / BW / SF / CR. Firmware ranges: 400–2500 MHz, 7.8–500 kHz, SF 5–12, CR 4/5–4/8.
mynode_lora_apply_preset_body: Radio settings affect every message the node sends and receives. Mismatched settings mean no traffic until the network agrees.
mynode_lora_community_footer: Catalogue mirrors the MeshCore app's “Select Radio Settings” screen. If your local mesh isn't listed, use Advanced to set raw values.
mynode_lora_current_device_footer: What the paired node reports right now. Presets below come from the MeshCore community catalogue — pick the one your local mesh runs.
mynode_lora_suggested_footer: Based on your device locale. Confirm to apply — nothing changes until you do.
mynode_pair_a_node_footer: First-time pairing shows the system bonding dialog.
mynode_reboot_dialog_body: The node disconnects briefly while it restarts. Use this when the device looks stuck or for hardware without a physical reset button. On nodes without a battery-backed RTC this also clears a forward-drifted clock.
mynode_reconnect_footer: Reconnect to the previously-paired node. No PIN prompt — the bond is still cached.
mynode_wifi_dialog_body: Default port for companion_radio's SerialWifiInterface is 5000.
mynode_zero_hop_body: Neighbours only. Quiet — no flooding. Use when only nearby nodes need to learn your advert.
nodes_add_channel_scan_qr_subtitle: Point camera at a channel QR
nodes_add_channel_secret_footer: Secret is the channel's shared 16-byte AES key, written as 32 hex characters. For hashtag-style channels the secret derives from the name automatically — leave the hash sign in place if you'd rather use that path.
nodes_add_contact_pubkey_footer: Public key is the contact's 32-byte Ed25519 identity, written as 64 hex characters. Subsequent adverts will fill in GPS, signal, and any name updates.
nodes_add_contact_scan_qr_subtitle: Point camera at a contact QR
nodes_add_error_channel_store: Could not add the channel. A channel with the same name and a different key may already exist.
nodes_add_error_contact_store: Could not store the contact. Check the public key and try again.
nodes_add_error_pubkey: Public key must be 64 hex characters.
nodes_add_install_body_dm: Stock-firmware nodes need the contact in a slot before they can route a DM. Install now, or do it later from the contact's chat.
nodes_add_install_body_other: Stock-firmware nodes need the contact in a slot before the connector can address it (path-discovery, login, telemetry). Install now, or do it later from the node's detail page.
nodes_add_paste_no_url: Clipboard doesn't contain a meshcore:// link
nodes_add_scanner_prompt: Point at a meshcore:// QR code
nodes_admin_clock_ahead_hint: Clock is set ahead. Stock firmware refuses backward sync. Fix: run `clkreboot` from the CLI below — it resets the RTC to 2024-05-15 and reboots — then tap sync to push the current time.
nodes_admin_error_build: Couldn't prepare the request. Please try again.
nodes_admin_error_crypto: Encryption error. Please log in again or try again.
nodes_admin_error_dialect_mismatch: This action isn't supported on this repeater yet.
nodes_admin_error_generic: Something went wrong. Please try again.
nodes_admin_error_not_ready: Device is still syncing — please try again in a moment.
nodes_admin_error_seed_sign: Couldn't sign the telemetry request. Try Re-login or try again.
nodes_admin_error_storage: Local storage error. Please try again.
nodes_default_region_bullet1: • Flood packets will be scoped to the provided region.
nodes_default_region_bullet2: • Only repeaters allowing the region will forward scoped packets.
nodes_default_region_bullet3: • Leave this field blank for flood packets to remain unscoped.
nodes_default_region_disambig: This is the repeater's default flood scope. To set your own client default region, use Settings → Messaging.
nodes_default_region_info_banner: To use minimal mesh traffic, please use the refresh icons to request the info you need.
nodes_delete_dialog_body: Wipes contact, advert, repeater password, login state, path data, CLI scrollback, neighbours, status, telemetry. Direct messages kept.
nodes_detail_acl_footer: Login adds this connector to the repeater's ACL so path-discovery / neighbours / telemetry work. Leave the password empty to log in as a guest or re-attach an existing ACL entry, or enter the admin or guest password for more access.
nodes_detail_assign_region: Assign a region so repeaters forward your messages on the right channel. Without a matching region, region-scoped repeaters drop the message.
nodes_detail_directory_offline: Online directory is off — enable a source in Settings → Sharing → Directory.
nodes_detail_fw_too_old: Requires firmware v8+. Connected device reports v%1$d.
nodes_detail_login_hint_empty: Leave empty to attempt guest access, or to re-attach if your key is already in this repeater's access list.
nodes_detail_login_hint_overlength: A repeater password is limited to 15 characters — anything longer is truncated when you log in.
nodes_detail_path_refresh_hint: Refresh path or set a manual path first.
nodes_detail_rename_empty_hint: Leave empty to use the advertised name.
nodes_detail_unknown_body: No advert or contact row exists for this public key yet.
nodes_empty_msg_nothing: Nothing of this type has been heard yet.
nodes_empty_msg_repeaters: Nearby repeaters appear here as their adverts reach your paired node.
nodes_empty_msg_rooms: Room server adverts show up here.
nodes_empty_msg_users: Other users' adverts show up here once their packets reach your paired node.
nodes_filter_hops_hint: 0 = only nodes heard directly by the paired device. Nodes with unknown hop counts are always shown.
nodes_filter_online_directory_hint: Repeaters resolved via the online directory mix into the list when this is on. Turn off to see only nodes we've actually heard on radio.
nodes_manage_regions_affected: Used by %1$d channel%2$s and %3$d contact%4$s. They will revert to no region (plain Flood).
nodes_manage_regions_err_no_reply: No reply from repeater. Mesh may be busy.
nodes_manage_regions_no_affected: No channels or contacts use this region. Deletion cannot be undone.
nodes_manage_regions_save_changes_body: You have unsaved region changes. They are active on the repeater right now but will revert on next reboot unless saved.
nodes_manage_regions_snackbar_busy_retry: Repeater busy. Please wait a moment and try again.
nodes_manage_regions_snackbar_has_childr: Cannot delete — region has child regions.
nodes_manage_regions_snackbar_needs_newe: Repeater rejected command. May need newer firmware.
nodes_manage_regions_snackbar_region_cap: Cannot add region. May be at the 32-region cap.
nodes_manage_regions_snackbar_unexpected: Unexpected reply from repeater. See diagnostics.
nodes_manual_err_bad_bytes: Couldn't read those bytes. Use 6 hex characters per hop (e.g. 1faec3) and split with commas, spaces, or newlines.
nodes_manual_err_not_triple: Each hop is 3 bytes. Got %1$d total — either pad or trim.
nodes_manual_err_storage_rejected: Storage rejected the path. Make sure the contact still exists.
nodes_manual_footer: Bytes are stored at 3-byte width and trimmed to the active TX path-hash size at send time. Comma, space, and newline separators are all accepted.
nodes_manual_no_learned_path: (no learned path yet — Direct sends would go zero-hop)
nodes_manual_picker_placeholder: Search by name or hex prefix
nodes_manual_picker_type_to_search: Type to search by name or hex prefix.
nodes_manual_use_manual_path_hint: Send via the bytes below instead of the learned route. Leave the bytes empty to send zero-hop Direct.
nodes_qr_copy_fallback: Try copying the link below and sharing it as text.
nodes_qr_error_channel: The channel secret must be 16 bytes.
nodes_qr_error_contact: The public key must be 32 bytes and the type one of 1..4.
nodes_qr_subtitle_channel: Anyone scanning this QR joins the channel with the same shared key.
nodes_qr_subtitle_contact: Anyone scanning this QR adds the contact to their MeshCore client.
nodes_save_repeater_to_fav_body: Favourites pin this repeater for quicker access. You can change this any time via the star button below.
notif_channel_mesh_link_desc: Shows while KiekR is holding a BLE link to your node.
room_admin_access_footer: Sign in to access admin tools. The password is stored on this device after a successful login.
room_admin_acl_empty: No ACL entries — tap Refresh to load.
room_admin_acl_invalid_hex: Invalid pubkey hex — must be exactly 64 hex characters
room_admin_acl_paste_hint: 64 hex characters — the node's full public key.
room_admin_admin_placeholder: Admin tools load here — CLI and ACL editor coming in the next update.
room_admin_cli_busy: Another request is in flight — please try again.
room_admin_login_failed: Login failed — wrong password or room unreachable.
room_admin_login_password_hint: Password is limited to 15 characters — anything longer is truncated when you sign in.
settings_autoadd_app_distance_footer: Adverts that arrived via more than this many repeaters are not auto-added by the app. 0 = direct only; 64 = unlimited (firmware cap).
settings_autoadd_app_types_footer: When the app hears an advert of an enabled type, it adds the node to your local contacts so it appears in Chats / Nodes. Filtered types lose their ECDH cache, so DMs from them can't be decrypted unless you add the contact manually. Changes mirror to the connected device when present.
settings_autoadd_companion_distance_foot: Adverts that arrived via more than this many repeaters are not auto-added by the device. 0 = direct only; 64 = unlimited (firmware cap).
settings_autoadd_companion_types_footer: When the connected device hears an advert of an enabled type, it stores the node in its contact table — survives reboots and reaches the app on next drain.
settings_autoadd_connect_hint: Connect a node to push policy changes to your device.
settings_autoadd_full_footer: Off: new adverts are dropped. On: the oldest non-favourite is evicted. Favourites are kept.
settings_autoadd_not_supported_body: Your paired node's firmware doesn't expose the auto-add policy. Upgrade to MeshCore v8 or later to manage it from here.
settings_autoresolve_bg_lookups_footer: When on, KiekR may automatically issue directory lookups for missing context the app notices passively — unknown hops in a path, heard repeaters not yet in the directory, missing neighbour rows, and missing regions on a discover. When off (default), every directory lookup requires a tap.
settings_autoresolve_surface_heard_repea: Heard repeaters not in the directory
settings_autoresolve_surface_neighbours: Neighbour rows the device hasn't seen yet
settings_autoresolve_surfaces_footer: Lookups go to the KiekR directory only — never to a third-party host. Cache reads stay free; this toggle gates the network half.
settings_backup_encrypted_footer: All KiekR data (regions, repeater logins, manual paths, MQTT brokers, ...) plus the stock JSON, AES-256-GCM encrypted with a passphrase you choose. Use this between KiekR installs.
settings_backup_err_identity_refused: Identity import refused: %1$s. Channels and contacts still landed.
settings_backup_err_missing_passphrase: This bundle is encrypted. Enter the passphrase you set when exporting.
settings_backup_err_wrong_passphrase: Wrong passphrase, or the file is corrupt.
settings_backup_export_footer: MeshCore-compatible JSON. The stock MeshCore app reads this file cleanly. Channels + contacts + identity travel; messages do not.
settings_backup_import_done: Import done — %1$d identity, %2$d channels, %3$d regions, %4$d contacts, %5$d repeater logins.
settings_backup_import_footer: Restore from a .json or .mcbackup file. Imports default to Replace — your existing channels, contacts, and regions are wiped, then the bundle is inserted. Tap and hold Confirm for 5 seconds.
settings_backup_import_mode_merge: Existing rows are kept; only new ones are added.
settings_backup_import_mode_replace: Existing channels, contacts, and regions will be wiped, then replaced.
settings_backup_passphrase_advisory: This is NOT your BLE pairing PIN. It only protects this backup file — pick something memorable; KiekR cannot recover it.
settings_battery_anchors_footer: Voltages must be strictly increasing; percentages non-decreasing. Add anchor inserts at the midpoint of the largest gap.
settings_battery_curve_footer: Map device voltage to a percent reading. Override the per-variant default when your pack chemistry differs (e.g. LiFePO4, AA).
settings_battery_polling_footer: How often the app asks the device for battery voltage. Visible runs while the app is open; background while it's hidden. Faster cadence = fresher reading, more BLE traffic.
settings_battery_preview_footer: Solid line = curve you're saving. Dashed line = default chemistry curve for this variant. X axis: 2800–4400 mV. Y axis: 0–100%%.
settings_battery_warn_footer: Notify when the connected device drops to the warn percent. The re-arm gap is how far the percent must climb back above the threshold before the next warning can fire — widen it if your battery curve plateaus (LiFePO4, AA).
settings_channel_conflicts_empty_body: Every channel on your node matches your local list.
settings_channel_conflicts_explanation_k: Your node has this channel named "%1$s", but locally it's "%2$s". They share the same encryption key, so messages still work — pick a name to keep.
settings_channel_conflicts_explanation_n: Both your local list and your node have a channel called "%1$s", but with different encryption keys. Messages won't decrypt across them — pick which key is the real one.
settings_channel_conflicts_keep_local_ke: Keep local key (push it back to node)
settings_channel_detail_dialog_delete_bo: Removes the channel and its key from the database. This channel uses a custom key, so it cannot be re-derived from the name — removal is permanent unless you re-import the key (e.g. via QR).
settings_channel_detail_dialog_remove_bo: Hides %1$s from your chat list and channel settings. You can re-add it later by typing the name in Settings → Channels → Add channel by name — the SHA-256 key derives the same way.
settings_channel_detail_footer_custom_re: Custom-key channels can't be re-derived from the name. Removal drops the key from the database for good.
settings_channel_detail_footer_free_slot: Free a slot first by removing an installed channel.
settings_channel_detail_footer_hashtag_r: Hides the channel from your list. The SHA-256-derived key stays so re-typing the name re-adds it.
settings_channel_detail_footer_install: Stock firmware needs the channel key in a device slot to transmit.
settings_channel_detail_footer_remove_on: Removing only clears the device slot. The channel stays in your list and can be reinstalled.
settings_channel_detail_notify_footer: All = every inbound message. Mentions = matches `@%1$s` in decrypted plaintext. Off = silent.
settings_channel_detail_region_footer: Assign the region your repeater forwards. Without a matching region, repeaters drop the message.
settings_channels_add_error: Couldn't add channel — it may already exist or the name/key is invalid.
settings_channels_add_hint_key_public: Uses the fixed public channel key — no key needed.
settings_channels_add_hint_key_required: Key required — custom-name channels need a 32-char hex key.
settings_channels_add_hint_name_empty: Prefix with # for a hashtag channel, or use 'Public' for the preset.
settings_channels_conflict_body: Your node and your local list disagree on a channel — pick which side wins.
settings_channels_delete_body: Removes the channel and its key. You'll need to re-add it to receive again.
settings_channels_empty_message: Tap + to add one. Use the channel name to join hashtag channels, or import a 32-character key.
settings_channels_hidden_footer: Key kept. Unhide to receive again — or delete permanently.
settings_community_auth_footer_jwt: LetsMesh-style brokers expect a 3-part EdDSA JWT signed with your per-broker upload key. KiekR signs and refreshes it automatically; you only fill in the audience (the broker's hostname).
settings_community_auth_footer_none: Anonymous connect — works for development brokers without auth.
settings_community_auto_disable_body:
“%1$s” couldn't connect after several attempts and was turned off so it doesn't keep retrying with possibly wrong details.

Last error: %2$s
settings_community_community_map_footer: Mirrors heard repeater adverts to the public MeshCore community map. Wi-Fi only. At most one upload per repeater per day.
settings_community_mqtt_footer: MQTT only relays while you're paired with your device over Bluetooth. Your device's mesh identity is never uploaded — each broker uses its own generated keypair. Observer names always start with “KiekR_”.
settings_community_no_brokers: No brokers configured. Tap “Add broker” to forward raw LoRa packets to an analyzer.
settings_community_observer_footer: 3-letter airport code identifies your region on the analyzer page (e.g. HAM, JFK, MUC). The observer name shows up next to your packets — the “KiekR_” prefix is fixed.
settings_community_preset_picker_footer: LetsMesh and Meshcorenetz presets are pre-filled with the broker's hostname, port, and auth method. You only need to add your IATA region code and observer name.
settings_community_privacy_dialog_body: MQTT upload forwards every raw LoRa packet your device hears — including encrypted bytes you can't read yourself — to the broker you configure. Default off; you turn it on per broker. Each broker uses its own generated keypair so your device's mesh identity never leaves the device.
settings_community_region_map_footer: When on, KiekR uploads every repeater advert it hears over the air to map.kiekr.app, plus any regions or neighbour lists you retrieve from a repeater. Uploads are pseudonymous and not linked to your KiekR identity. Helps build a public map of where regions are used. Messages, contacts, and your location are never shared.
settings_community_val_err_auth_method: Auth method must be none / password / jwt
settings_community_val_err_iata_length: IATA must be exactly 3 letters (e.g. HAM)
settings_community_val_err_jwt_audience: JWT auth needs an audience (e.g. mqtt-eu-v1.letsmesh.net)
settings_community_val_err_password_auth: Password auth needs both username and password
settings_community_val_err_transport: Transport must be tcp / ws / wss
settings_community_wifi_only_footer: Pauses uploads on cellular and resumes on WiFi. Saves mobile data.
settings_customization_edge_footer: Anything below Workable. Colour the marginal links so they stand out.
settings_customization_strong_footer: SNR readings at or above this cut-off use the Strong colour.
settings_customization_workable_footer: Readings between this cut-off and Strong use the Workable colour.
settings_directory_builtin_hint: Preconfigured rows can't be renamed or have their URL changed — toggle them off if you don't want to use them, or add a new source.
settings_directory_cache_footer: Removes every contact row imported from the online directory plus the on-disk prefix cache. Wire-heard contacts are untouched.
settings_directory_online_footer: Master switch. When off, no directory lookups run regardless of which sources below are enabled. Cached resolutions still serve. Enable a source below to start fresh lookups.
settings_directory_reset_confirm_body: Removes every contact row imported from the online directory plus the on-disk prefix cache. Wire-heard contacts are untouched.
settings_directory_sources_footer: Lookups walk every enabled source in rank order; the first source that returns a candidate wins. api.kiekr.app is privacy-preserving (tier-A repeaters only). Meshcorenetz Analyzer is broader — falls through naturally for nodes the KiekR backend doesn't have. Reorder with the up/down arrows; tap the pencil to edit per-source max distance per hop.
settings_help_bug_report_footer: Bundles the app's recent log so you can send it to the developer via Telegram, Signal, mail, or any other share target. Includes your device model, Android version, app build, and paired-node firmware (semver + protocol level). No message contents or full identity keys are included.
settings_help_logs_email_prompt: Describe the bug in a sentence or two:
settings_notifications_adverts_footer: Notify when a node of the selected type comes on-air. Adverts are noisy by default; enable Repeater if you want to know about new repeaters as they appear.
settings_notifications_discovery_footer: Notify on first traffic this session for each channel you haven't added yet (like #test or #ping). Lets you discover active channels without re-notifying for every message.
settings_notifications_discovery_label: Notify when traffic arrives on a never-added channel
settings_notifications_dm_footer: Notify on every inbound DM. DMs are cryptographically authenticated — turning this off only suppresses the notification; the message still arrives in your Chats list.
settings_notifications_master_footer: Master switch. When off, KiekR posts no notifications for messages, channels, or adverts. The per-type settings below are kept.
settings_notifications_per_channel_foote: Each channel has its own all / mention / off setting. Mention matches `@<your_name>` in decrypted plaintext.
settings_notifications_per_channel_hint: Open Channels settings to configure per-channel notifications.
settings_notifications_system_footer: Use Android's notification channels (System Settings → Apps → KiekR → Notifications) for sound, vibration, and Do Not Disturb. KiekR doesn't reinvent those.
settings_pathlookup_cache_footer:
Persistent learning aid for prefix collisions: once the deterministic picker has chosen a winner, the (prefix, hop, pubkey) is bumped so the next tie at the same prefix breaks the same way.

Cache TTL — how long an entry survives without being re-used. Default 30 days, clamp 1..=180.

Tiebreaker margin — how close two candidates' metrics must be (in %%) before the cache breaks the tie. 0 disables the cache fallback (only exact ties qualify); 20 is the upper bound. Default 5 %%.
settings_pathlookup_directory_moved_foot: Looking for the directory source picker? It moved to Settings → Sharing → Directory.
settings_pathlookup_encoding_footer_lock: Multi-byte hops require companion firmware v1.15.0 or newer. Your companion reports an older version — locked to 1 Byte until you upgrade. Your previous preference is preserved and will reactivate after the upgrade.
settings_pathlookup_encoding_footer_supp: Bytes per hop appended to the path on new outbound packets we originate. Higher values reduce name collisions but may not be forwarded by older repeater firmware. Learned paths to known contacts keep the width they were received at — this setting only affects flood and anon-reply paths. Experimental above 1 Byte; default 1.
settings_region_discovery_directory_off: Online directory is off — enable a source in Settings → Sharing → Directory.
settings_region_discovery_no_repeaters: No repeaters heard yet. Tap Scan to try.
settings_region_discovery_scan_footer: Sends DISCOVER_REQ; listens 20 s for nearby repeaters.
settings_region_picker_footer: Hide regions you don't use under Settings → Regions to keep this list short.
settings_regions_active_footer: Tap the menu on a region to hide it or delete it. Hidden regions stay in the database (and keep annotating incoming traffic) but disappear from channel pickers.
settings_regions_add_dialog_hint: Must match a region your repeater is configured for.
settings_regions_default_scope_unsupport: The paired node reports firmware %1$s (companion protocol level %2$s); default region requires protocol level 11. Update to a firmware build that includes the v11 opcodes.
settings_regions_delete_confirm_body: Removes the region from your device. You can re-add it later by name.
settings_regions_empty_message: Tap + above to add a region by name, or scan a nearby repeater for the regions it forwards.
settings_regions_remove_in_use_both: "%1$s" is still assigned to %2$d channel(s) and %3$d contact(s). Removing it resets them to no region (plain Flood).
settings_regions_remove_in_use_channels: "%1$s" is still assigned to %2$d channel(s). Removing it resets them to no region (plain Flood).
settings_regions_remove_in_use_contacts: "%1$s" is still assigned to %2$d contact(s). Removing it resets them to no region (plain Flood).
settings_regions_scope_footer_none: Unscoped sends go out as plain Flood. Every repeater on the mesh will rebroadcast them.
settings_regions_scope_footer_set: Unscoped sends are silently transport-coded to %1$s. Only repeaters configured for that region will rebroadcast them.
settings_regions_scope_footer_unknown: Pick a region to set as the paired node's default scope, or None for plain Flood. The setting is stored on the node and survives reboots.
settings_regions_set_default_body: This setting is stored on the paired node and survives reboots. It also affects every other client that pairs with this node.
settings_regions_toast_already_in_list_u: Region %1$s already in your list — unhid it
settings_retries_channel_delay_footer:
How long to wait for a repeater echo on a channel message before surfacing the retry glyph. Default 10 s. Lower for fast meshes where you want to retry sooner; raise on slow / far-flung meshes where the first echo can take 20–30 s.

DM retry delay is fixed at 140 s — it tracks the wire-level auto-retry ladder duration. Exposing it would surface the manual retry button before the auto-retry chain finishes, which would interleave manual + auto retries and confuse the bubble's attempt counter.
settings_retries_manual_cap_footer: Hard cap on user-initiated retries per outgoing message — the orange ↻ glyph on un-acked bubbles. Once you hit the cap the glyph greys out and stops accepting taps. Default 5 (matches the auto-retry ladder: 3× direct + 2× flood).
settings_retries_owner_info_footer: How many times the app re-sends an owner-info request if no reply arrives, and how long it waits between attempts. Default 1 retry, 8 s interval. One retry is usually enough — the request goes to a specific repeater, so the link quality is predictable.
settings_retries_region_discovery_footer: How many times the app re-sends a region-discovery request if no reply arrives, and how long it waits between attempts. Default 2 retries, 8 s interval. Raise the retry count on noisy meshes; raise the interval on slow / congested links.
settings_tools_autosave_subtitle: Slower but never forgets to persist.
settings_tools_autostart_subtitle: Allow KiekR to run in the background. The exact screen depends on your phone maker.
settings_tools_background_footer: Android suspends app networking when the screen is off. Exempt KiekR from battery optimization — and from your phone maker’s auto-start limits — so the companion link survives in the background.
settings_tools_battery_opt_subtitle: Set KiekR to “Don’t optimize” so it keeps the link alive in the background.
settings_tools_cli_timeout_subtitle: How long to wait for a repeater CLI reply before giving up.
settings_tools_neighbour_footer: Retries is how many extra attempts core-lib makes per GET_NEIGHBOURS page if the firmware doesn't reply within the per-attempt window — handy on flaky BLE / radio links where a page times out. The window scales with the page size, so bumping it gives the extra attempts the time they need. The page size itself is fixed at the firmware's buffer ceiling. Default %1$d.
settings_tools_region_admin_footer: Tuning knobs for the region admin CLI used when pushing region lists to repeaters. Defaults are conservative; lower the CLI timeout only if your repeaters are reliably fast.
ui_connection_banner_disconnected: No node connected — tap to pair
ui_directory_notice_body: KiekR can look up an unknown repeater by its hash via online directories — handy when a path hop shows up as a bare hex prefix. It stays off until you switch it on under Settings → Sharing → Directory.
ui_identity_changed_body:
This node uses a different identity than the one you had active.

Previous: %1$s…
Now: %2$s… · %3$s

DM history is scoped per identity, so conversations under the previous identity won't appear here. Peers who had your previous public key need to add this new one before they can decrypt new DMs.
ui_install_consent_body: %1$s isn't on your connected node yet. Add it now? If your node's %2$s list is full, the oldest non-favourite %2$s will be replaced.
ui_map_consent_body:
KiekR can upload every repeater advert it hears over the air to map.kiekr.app, plus any regions or neighbour lists you retrieve from a repeater. Uploads are pseudonymous and not linked to your KiekR identity. Messages, contacts, and your location are never shared.

You can change this any time in Settings → Community upload.
ui_pathhash_reconcile_body: Your app is set to %1$d Byte, this node is set to %2$d Byte. Pick which should apply.
ui_region_reconcile_body: Your app is set to %1$s, this node is set to %2$s. Pick which should apply.
nodes_admin_reboot_body: The repeater restarts and briefly drops off the mesh. It sends no confirmation.
nodes_detail_needs_login_footer: Log in (guest) for stats, neighbours, telemetry & firmware · admin for config & CLI.
nodes_admin_clkreboot_body: Resets the repeater's clock to a baseline (2024-05-15) and reboots it, then tap the sync icon to push the current time. The reboot sends no confirmation.
nodes_admin_position_hint: Sets the repeater's advertised position. Enter decimal degrees; a map picker is a planned follow-up.
nodes_cli_confirm_reboot: The repeater drops off the mesh while it restarts. No reply is sent.
nodes_cli_confirm_poweroff: The repeater powers down and won't return until physically restarted. No reply is sent.
nodes_cli_confirm_ota: Puts the repeater into firmware-update mode.
nodes_cli_confirm_clear_stats: Resets the repeater's traffic counters to zero.
nodes_manual_picker_empty_desc: Repeaters and room servers you've saved as contacts appear here.
path_anchor_live_fallback: Anchored on your current location — no position was stored for this message.
settings_blocklist_empty_body: Long-press a message in a channel and choose Block to hide every message from that sender. Channel sender names aren't authenticated, so blocking is by name only.
settings_blocklist_section_footer: Tap the trash icon to unblock. Their past messages reappear in every channel. Blocking matches the exact name shown — anyone can pick that name, so this won't stop a determined poster from renaming.
settings_community_coverage_footer: When on, KiekR records which regions (and, where possible, which repeaters) it hears in a coarse map cell of about half a kilometre, and periodically uploads the per-cell totals over Wi-Fi. Your exact position never leaves the phone — only the cell — and the map only ever shows what was heard there, never who heard it. Pseudonymous and separate from your KiekR identity; turn it off any time.
settings_community_coverage_precise_hint: Coverage needs precise location to place what you hear in the right map cell. Allow precise location for KiekR — otherwise coverage collects nothing on this phone.
mynode_known_companions_footer: Tap a companion to reconnect. Swipe to delete it from this list.
mynode_scan_searching_hint: Make sure your node is powered on and close by.
ping_detail_blurb: Measure round-trip time and uplink / downlink signal to this repeater.
ping_settings_footer: A ping fires N one-hop probes to a repeater and reports round-trip time plus uplink / downlink signal. Samples are sent sequentially (interval apart); each waits up to the timeout for a reply. Defaults: 3 samples, 2000 ms interval, 5000 ms timeout.
traceroute_settings_footer: A traceroute probes a route you pick hop-by-hop and reports per-hop signal. Single-shot (no retries) — you chose the path. Default 30 s timeout.
traceroute_add_hops_hint: Add repeaters in the order the probe should visit them.
traceroute_reachability_hint: The first repeater must hear you and you must hear the last one's reply — pick a path you know is reachable.
traceroute_no_response: No response — the path may not be reachable.
tools_traceroute_blurb: Probe a route you pick hop-by-hop and read the signal at each hop.
traceroute_search_hint: Search by name or hex prefix
chat_compose_gate_no_node: No node connected — connect a companion to send.
chat_compose_gate_identity: This DM belongs to %1$s. You're connected as %2$s.
settings_language_footer: KiekR follows your device language by default. To pick a different language just for KiekR, open the system settings and choose one there.
settings_appearance_footer: Automatic follows your device's Light/Dark setting. Choose Light or Dark to override it just for KiekR.
live_traffic_empty_desc: Every frame your node hears shows up here — nothing is filtered out.
live_traffic_footer: Unfiltered — decoded, encrypted-not-for-us, unknown, and malformed frames all appear.
live_traffic_hero_sub: shows every received frame — even what can't be read
mynode_companion_delete_confirm_body: Removes this companion from the list. If it is currently connected, the link is dropped first.
chat_retry_duplicate_body: Your node already transmitted this message. No confirmation was heard back, but receivers in range may have gotten it — sending again shows it to them twice.
nodes_bulk_delete_device_note: Contacts installed on the connected node are removed there as well.
settings_backup_import_done_v2: Import done — %1$d identity, %2$d channels, %3$d regions, %4$d contacts, %5$d repeater logins, %6$d favorites, %7$d blocked senders, %8$d settings.
settings_backup_warn_newer_schema: Backup was written by a newer app version (schema %1$s) — applied best effort.
settings_backup_warn_empty_bundle: The file contained no importable data.
chat_detail_receptions_lower_bound: Counts are a lower bound — busy links can drop copies before they reach the app.
